description: MeshFaultInjection allows you to test the resiliency of your services by injecting faults like delays, connection aborts, and response bandwidth limits into the traffic. This is useful for chaos testing and validating that your applications handle failures gracefully.

apiVersion.description:
APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.
Servers should convert recognized schemas to the latest internal value, and
may reject unrecognized values.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#resources

kind.description:
Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.
Servers may infer this from the endpoint the client submits requests to.
Cannot be updated.
In CamelCase.
More info: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/devel/sig-architecture/api-conventions.md#types-kinds

spec.description: Spec is the specification of the Kuma MeshFaultInjection resource.

from.description: From list makes a match between clients and corresponding configurations

default.description:
Default is a configuration specific to the group of destinations referenced in
'targetRef'

http.description: Http allows to define list of Http faults between dataplanes.

items.description: FaultInjection defines the configuration of faults between dataplanes.

abort.description:
Abort defines a configuration of not delivering requests to destination
service and replacing the responses from destination dataplane by
predefined status code

httpStatus.description: HTTP status code which will be returned to source side

percentage.description:
Percentage of requests on which abort will be injected, has to be
either int or decimal represented as string.

delay.description: Delay defines configuration of delaying a response from a destination

percentage.description:
Percentage of requests on which delay will be injected, has to be
either int or decimal represented as string.

value.description: The duration during which the response will be delayed

responseBandwidth.description:
ResponseBandwidth defines a configuration to limit the speed of
responding to the requests

limit.description:
Limit is represented by value measure in Gbps, Mbps, kbps, e.g.
10kbps

percentage.description:
Percentage of requests on which response bandwidth limit will be
either int or decimal represented as string.

targetRef.description:
TargetRef is a reference to the resource that represents a group of
destinations.

labels.description:
Labels are used to select group of MeshServices that match labels. Either Labels or
Name and Namespace can be used.

mesh.description: Mesh is reserved for future use to identify cross mesh resources.

name.description:
Name of the referenced resource. Can only be used with kinds: `MeshService`,
`MeshServiceSubset` and `MeshGatewayRoute`

namespace.description:
Namespace specifies the namespace of target resource. If empty only resources in policy namespace
will be targeted.

proxyTypes.description:
ProxyTypes specifies the data plane types that are subject to the policy. When not specified,
all data plane types are targeted by the policy.

sectionName.description:
SectionName is used to target specific section of resource.
For example, you can target port from MeshService.ports[] by its name. Only traffic to this port will be affected.

tags.description:
Tags used to select a subset of proxies by tags. Can only be used with kinds
`MeshSubset` and `MeshServiceSubset`

rules.description: Rules defines inbound fault injection configuration

http.description: Http allows to define list of Http faults between dataplanes.

items.description: FaultInjection defines the configuration of faults between dataplanes.

abort.description:
Abort defines a configuration of not delivering requests to destination
service and replacing the responses from destination dataplane by
predefined status code

httpStatus.description: HTTP status code which will be returned to source side

percentage.description:

either int or decimal represented as string.

percentage.description:

either int or decimal represented as string.

value.description: The duration during which the response will be delayed

responseBandwidth.description:
ResponseBandwidth defines a configuration to limit the speed of
responding to the requests

limit.description:

10kbps

percentage.description:
Percentage of requests on which response bandwidth limit will be
either int or decimal represented as string.

matches.description: Matches defines list of matches for which fault injection will be applied

sni.description: SNI defines a matcher configuration for matching by SNI value carried on the TLS connection

type.description: Type defines how to match traffic by SNI. Only `Exact` is supported.

value.description: Value is the SNI carried on the TLS connection that needs to match for the configuration to be applied

spiffeID.description: SpiffeID defines a matcher configuration for SpiffeID matching

type.description: Type defines how to match incoming traffic by SpiffeID. `Exact` or `Prefix` are allowed.

value.description: Value is SpiffeID of a client that needs to match for the configuration to be applied

targetRef.description:
TargetRef is a reference to the resource the policy takes an effect on.
The resource could be either a real store object or virtual resource
defined inplace.

labels.description:
Labels are used to select group of MeshServices that match labels. Either Labels or
Name and Namespace can be used.
